recalls.Description: Yamaha has issued a recall for its 2021-2025 Drive 2 Personal Transportation Vehicles (PTVs), which include both gas and electric golf cars. These vehicles, available in various colors, may lack essential stop lights, leading to a significant risk of serious injury or death in the event of a crash. The model name and serial number can be found on the sides of the vehicle, with serial numbers located in different areas depending on the model. If you need help finding your serial number, reach out to Yamaha for assistance.
recalls.Title: Yamaha Recalls 2021-2025 Golf Car Vehicles Due to Crash Hazard Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For more information, consumers can contact Yamaha at 866-747-4027 from 8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET daily, via email at ygc_callcenter@yamaha-motor.com, or visit https://www.yamahamotorsports.com/Content.php?content=cpsc-recalls or https://yamaha-motor.com/ and select 'CPSC Recalls' at the bottom of the page.

Images.Caption: Recalled Yamaha Drive 2 J2D Super Hauler

Retailers.Name: Available through Yamaha dealers nationwide from June 2020 to July 2025, priced between $8,300 and $12,900.

Importers.Name: Yamaha Golf Car Company, of Kennesaw, Georgia

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious injury or death due to lack of stop lights

Remedies.Name: Free installation of stoplights at authorized Yamaha dealers or at the consumer's location

recalls.metaDescription: Yamaha recalls 2021-2025 golf car vehicles due to crash hazard. Contact Yamaha for free stoplight installation.

recalls.Description: Yamaha is recalling its 2017-2024 DR2A and DR2E Personal Transportation Vehicles (PTVs) due to a serious crash hazard. The affected vehicles have serial numbers starting with J0D, J0E, or J2D and were available in various colors. You can find the serial number on the handlebars located on the left side of the rear seat row.
recalls.Title: Yamaha Recalls 2017-2024 Golf Car Vehicles Due to Potential Crash Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Yamaha at 866-747-4027, available from 8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET daily. You can also reach them via email at ygc_callcenter@yamaha-motor.com or visit https://www.yamahamotorsports.com/Content.php?content=cpsc-recalls or https://yamaha-motor.com/ and click on "CPSC Recalls" for further details.

Images.Caption: Recalled DR2A and DR2E Golf Cars

Retailers.Name: Available at Yamaha dealers across the U.S. from June 2018 to July 2025, priced between $9,500 and $13,000.

Importers.Name: Yamaha Golf Car Company, of Kennesaw, Georgia

Hazards.Name: Risk of serious injury or death due to potential brake failure.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use immediately and contact an authorized Yamaha Golf Car dealer for free installation of new brake shoes and pads. Yamaha will also arrange transportation or on-site installation for those unable to visit a dealer.

recalls.metaDescription: Yamaha recalls 2017-2024 golf car vehicles due to crash hazards. Learn how to get free brake repairs and stay safe.

recalls.Description: Professional's Choice Sports Medicine Products has issued a recall for specific Swivel Port mouthpiece equine bits from the Bob Avila collection. The affected models include the Avila Santa Maria Swivel Port (model AVB-171), featuring a 7 ¼" shank with decorative silver accents; the Avila Byron Swivel Port (model AVB-612), which has an 8" Gunmetal shank; the Avila Phoenix Swivel Port (model AVB-621), an 8" cheek adorned with a silver and copper sunflower design; and the Avila Sunflower Swivel Port (model AVB-222), a 7" shank with a floral decoration in silver and copper. These bits may break during use, posing a fall hazard to riders.
recalls.Title: Recall of Professional's Choice Equine Bits Due to Safety Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should contact Professional's Choice Sports Medicine Products Inc. at 800-331-9421 from 8 a.m. to 4 p.m. CT Monday through Friday, email info@profchoice.com, or visit https://profchoice.com/recalls or https://profchoice.com and navigate to the 'Recalls' section under Customer Care.

Products.Name: Avila Santa Maria Swivel Port Bit

Images.Caption: Recalled Avila Santa Maria Swivel Port equine bit model number AVB-171
Recalled Avila Sunflower Swivel Port equine bit model number AVB-222
Recalled Avila Byron Swivel Port equine bit model number AVB-612
Recalled Avila Phoenix Swivel Port equine bit with model number AVB-621

Injuries.Name: The company has received five reports of bits breaking.No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at horse-riding supply stores across the country and online at profchoice.com from January 2022 through March 2025, priced between $120 and $160.

Importers.Name: Professional's Choice Sports Medicine Products Inc., of Oklahoma City, Oklahoma

Hazards.Name: Risk of bit breaking during use, leading to potential fall hazard.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease using the recalled bits and reach out to Professional's Choice for a full refund or a free replacement. Upon receiving the returned product, Professional's Choice will issue a refund via check or send a replacement, based on the consumer's choice. A pre-paid shipping label will be provided for returns. Additionally, consumers may contact the original retailer for a refund.

recalls.metaDescription: Professional's Choice recalls specific equine bits due to a fall hazard. Stop using them and contact for a refund or replacement.

recalls.Description: Total Saddle Fit is recalling its Shoulder Relief Cinches, which are intended to secure saddles on horses. These leather cinches are available in black and brown, measuring between 24 and 36 inches. The Total Saddle Fit branding is found near the buckle. This recall specifically affects cinches sold before August 2021, identifiable by a round buckle and the absence of center elastic.
recalls.Title: Total Saddle Fit Recalls Shoulder Relief Cinches Due to Fall and Injury Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Total Saddle Fit via email at care@totalsaddlefit.com or visit https://www.totalsaddlefit.com/recall. You can also find more details by clicking on 'Safety Recall' at the bottom of their website.

Images.Caption: Recalled Shoulder Relief Cinch in brown and black color

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 19 reports of the cinch coming apart and the saddle falling off, including one report of a rider fracturing their ribs.

Retailers.Name: Available online at totalsaddlefit.com and through retailers such as Hitching Post Tack Shop, Tack & Western Wear, and Saddlery Direct across the country from August 2015 to August 2021 for approximately $140.

Importers.Name: Total Saddle Fit, LLC, of Jamul, California

Hazards.Name: Risk of cinch detachment leading to saddle falls, posing potential injury to riders.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are urged to stop using the recalled cinch and reach out to Total Saddle Fit for a free replacement. Instructions for returning the original cinch will be provided, including a pre-paid shipping label.

recalls.metaDescription: Total Saddle Fit recalls Shoulder Relief Cinches due to safety risks. Stop using immediately and contact for a free replacement.

recalls.Description:
Torquay eTrading LLC is recalling Essential Values Golf Grip Solvent and Grip & Rip re-gripping kits due to a violation of the Federal Hazardous Substances Act. These products, which were sold in 8-ounce white bottles with blue labels, were marketed as 'Safe & Non-Toxic' but lack necessary cautionary statements regarding petroleum distillates, posing a poisoning hazard to consumers. The kits included 14 strips of golf tape and were previously available in various pack sizes including 2-pack, 4-pack, and 6-pack options.

Consumers are urged to stop using the affected products immediately and reach out to Torquay eTrading for guidance on obtaining a free corrected label. The company, along with retailers like Amazon, eBay, and Walmart, is proactively contacting known purchasers to address the issue.
recalls.URL: https://www.cpsc.gov/Recalls/2024/Torquay eTrading Recalls Essential Values Golf Grip Solvent and-Golf-Grip-Kits-Due-to-Risk-of-Poisoning-Violation-of-the-Federal-Hazardous-Substances-Act
recalls.Title: Recall of Essential Values Golf Grip Solvent and Grip Kits Due to Poisoning Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Torquay eTrading LLC at 347-587-8964 from 8 a.m. to 4 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email at recall@essentialvalues.us, or visit https://www.essentialvalues.us/pages/recall or https://www.essentialvalues.us/ and click 'Recall' at the top for more details.

Products.Name: Essential Values Golf Grip Solvent
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 26,000 (In addition, about 3,400 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Grip and Rip Re-Gripping Solution Kit with 14 Strips of Grip Tape
Back of the Recalled Golf Grip Solution

Retailers.Name: Available online at Amazon.com, eBay.com, Walmart.com, and www.essentialvalues.com from December 2017 through May 2024, priced around $9, with the kit including golf grip tape costing approximately $13.

Distributors.Name: Torquay eTrading LLC (dba Essential Values), of New York (formerly sold by Mocer LLC and Bright Ventures LLC)

Hazards.Name: Risk of poisoning due to misleading labeling as 'Safe and Non-Toxic' without required cautionary statements.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the products and contact Torquay eTrading for a free corrected label.

recalls.metaDescription: Essential Values Golf Grip Solvent and Grip Kits recalled due to poisoning risk. Stop using and contact Torquay eTrading for a free corrected label.

recalls.Description: Excalibur Crossbow is recalling its C2 Crank cocking aid designed for crossbows due to safety concerns. These devices, featuring two plastic cocking hooks, can break during use, leading to potential impact injuries and lacerations for users and nearby individuals. The C2 Crank is identifiable by the 'Excalibur Crossbow' logo and is sold in black. The part number 2199 is also indicated on the packaging.
recalls.Title: Excalibur Crossbow C2 Crank Cocking Aids Recalled Due to Injury Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the C2 Crank immediately and contact Excalibur Crossbow at 888-689-1289, Monday through Thursday from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, or via email at warranty@excaliburcrossbow.com. More information is available at www.excaliburcrossbow.com under the 'Recalls' section or directly at https://excaliburcrossbow.com/c2-crank-recall/.

Products.Name: Excalibur C2 Crank Cocking Aid
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 15,000 (In addition, about 3,920 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Excalibur Crossbow C2 Crank with cocking hooks

Injuries.Name: Excalibur has received 538 reports of the C2 Crank cocking aid hooks breaking during use, resulting in plastic projectiles flying and hitting consumers, including one injury where a consumer reportedly suffered a permanent eye injury.

Manufacturers.Name: Excalibur Crossbow US LLC, of Canada

Retailers.Name: Available at Bass Pro Shops, Cabela's, and various retailers nationwide, as well as online at excaliburcrossbow.com, ebay.com, and amazon.com from January 2008 to December 2021, priced around $190.

Hazards.Name: Risk of impact injury and laceration from breaking cocking hooks

Remedies.Name: Free replacement rope and hooks available upon request

recalls.metaDescription: Recall of Excalibur Crossbow C2 Crank cocking aids due to injury risks. Stop using immediately and contact for free replacements.
